old_claim.note: Port Customs Ledgers provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Merchant ship registration was administered through Custom Houses and port officials, making port customs recordkeeping the enabling institutional record practice.

source_shape.source_locator: Royal Museums Greenwich explains ship registration and Custom House records, including 1661 registration with Custom Houses and 1786 registration with Customs in the home port.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source places merchant ship registration inside Custom House and home-port Customs record systems.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports port_customs_ledgers as the enabling recordkeeping context because the registry was administered through customs offices rather than through voyage logbooks or insurance claims offices.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: merchant_marine_registry keeps a direct port_customs_ledgers dependency, now source-backed and scoped to Custom House ship registration.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped away from Custom House ship registration to a private shipping directory.
A stronger source showed merchant ship registration was administered independently of Customs or port registry offices.
The port_customs_ledgers node were narrowed to exclude port-based official customs recordkeeping.
short_reason: The registration source explicitly places the practice in Custom House and port Customs records.
